steps.title: Expand both sides
steps.content: Distribute on the left: $$6(x+15)+4x = 6x + 90 + 4x = 10x + 90$$ Distribute on the right: $$10(x+9) = 10x + 90$$ The equation becomes: $$10x + 90 = 10x + 90$$

steps.title: Interpret the result
steps.content: Subtracting 10x + 90 from both sides gives 0 = 0, a statement that is always true regardless of x. Every real number satisfies the original equation.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Exactly one’: Exactly one solution requires the equation to collapse to x = (some specific number). Here both sides are identical for all x - no single unique value. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Exactly two’: A linear equation cannot have exactly two solutions - it has zero, one, or infinitely many. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Infinitely many’: Since 10x + 90 = 10x + 90 is always true, every real number x is a solution. Infinitely many solutions. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Zero’: Zero solutions requires a contradiction like 0 = 5. Here we get 0 = 0, a tautology - not a contradiction. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: After expanding, both sides equal 10x + 90 identically, so every value of x works. That confirms C is correct.
